commands.review_summary:
This PR changes MCP tool-result materialization to coerce non-text/image MCP ContentBlock variants into AgentToolResult text blocks and adds regression coverage.

PR surface: Source +36, Tests +66. Total +102 across 2 files.

Reproducibility: yes. at source level: current main accepts MCP CallToolResult.content as AgentToolResult.content while the MCP SDK contract includes non-text/image blocks. I did not run a live MCP/Anthropic reproduction in this read-only review.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] No ClawSweeper repair is appropriate because the remaining blocker is contributor real behavior proof or maintainer live validation, not a concrete code defect in the patch.

target.title: fix(agents): coerce non-text/image MCP tool-result blocks to text (fixes #90710)
target.body:
## Summary

MCP `CallToolResult.content[]` can include `resource_link`, `resource`, and `audio` blocks (MCP SDK `ContentBlock` union), but `toAgentToolResult` in `agent-bundle-mcp-materialize.ts` cast that array straight into `AgentToolResult.content`, whose contract is only `(TextContent | ImageContent)[]`. That cast lied to the type system, so the bad blocks flowed downstream where the Anthropic provider/transport "non-text ⇒ image" branch built an `image` block with `undefined` `data`/`media_type`. Anthropic rejects that with **HTTP 400**, and because the malformed block stays in conversation history, **every subsequent turn replays it and 400s too** — the session is permanently poisoned until history is reset.

- Fix at the **materialize boundary** so the `text`/`image` contract stays honest, instead of patching each downstream provider converter. This fixes **all providers**, not just Anthropic.
- Pass through valid images unchanged; coerce `resource_link` → `[title|name] uri`, `resource` → `resource.text ?? uri`, `audio` → `[audio <mime>]`, and malformed/unknown blocks → stringified text.
- Out of scope: provider-side `convertContentBlocks` keep their honest `text|image` signatures; they no longer receive wider MCP blocks, so they are intentionally untouched.

Discriminated-union handling means a future MCP SDK block type surfaces here rather than silently mis-coercing.

## Linked context

Closes #90710

Reporter: @RanSHammer (clear repro + root-cause diagnosis).

## Real behavior proof (required for external PRs)

- Behavior or issue addressed: MCP tool returning a non-image, non-text block (e.g. `resource_link` from an attachment tool on a `.docx`/`.pdf`) no longer produces a malformed Anthropic `image` block; it becomes a text block, so no 400 and no poisoned history.
- Real environment tested: local source checkout (Node 22.22.2), worktree off `origin/main`.
- Exact steps or command run after this patch:
  - `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/agents/agent-bundle-mcp-tools.materialize.test.ts`
  - `node scripts/run-tsgo.mjs -p tsconfig.core.json` and `-p test/tsconfig/tsconfig.test.src.json`
  - `oxfmt --check` and `scripts/run-oxlint.mjs` on the changed files
- Evidence after fix (terminal capture): `Test Files 1 passed (1) / Tests 13 passed (13)`; both `tsgo` lanes `EXIT=0`; oxfmt "All matched files use the correct format"; oxlint `EXIT=0`.
- Observed result after fix: a mixed `CallToolResult` (text + two `resource_link` + two `resource` + `audio` + `image`) materializes to six text blocks plus the one real image block; a malformed `{type:"image"}` block (no source) becomes text instead of an empty image block.
- What was not tested: a live end-to-end roundtrip against the Anthropic API with a real remote MCP server (no MCP server with `resource_link` output wired up in this environment). The regression is reproduced and locked at the materialize boundary via unit tests that assert the exact normalized blocks.
- Proof limitations or environment constraints: no live MCP/Anthropic credentials in this checkout; covered by deterministic unit tests at the boundary that produced the malformed block.

## Tests and validation

Which commands did you run?

- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/agents/agent-bundle-mcp-tools.materialize.test.ts` → 13 passed
- `node scripts/run-tsgo.mjs -p tsconfig.core.json` → exit 0
- `node scripts/run-tsgo.mjs -p test/tsconfig/tsconfig.test.src.json` → exit 0
- `oxfmt --check` + `scripts/run-oxlint.mjs` on changed files → clean

What regression coverage was added or updated?

Two new cases in `agent-bundle-mcp-tools.materialize.test.ts`:
- `resource_link` / `resource` (text + blob) / `audio` blocks coerce to text while a valid `image` passes through.
- A malformed `image` block (missing base64 source) coerces to text instead of an empty image block.
